content:
# Master Daily Prep List

**Tool ID:** T179-01
**Block:** B179 (Daily Prep List Protocol)
**Domain:** Operations & Workflow (D05_operations_workflow)
**Level:** L1
**Format:** Checklist
**Role:** Head Barista
**Time Required:** 15–20 minutes
**Version:** 1.0

---
## Welcome

A shift without a prep list is a shift that starts behind. This master prep list is the daily roadmap for getting the cafe ready for service — every task, every station, every item, checked off in order of priority. When this list is complete, your first customer walks into a cafe that is fully loaded and ready to perform.

Print it. Pin it. Do not open the doors until every box is ticked.

**Built for Head Barista working in Operations & Workflow.**
Use it. Adapt it. Make it yours.

---

## Purpose

A comprehensive daily preparation checklist covering every station and task required before opening service — from espresso machine warm-up and grinder dialling-in to food prep, display setup, and cleaning station readiness. Eliminates reliance on memory and ensures consistency regardless of who opens.

This tool supports **Daily Prep List Protocol** (B179) and is designed to be practical, printable, and immediately useful in a working cafe environment.

---

## How to Use This Tool

**Who:** Head Barista (or opening team leader)
**Time Required:** 15–20 minutes to work through; prep tasks themselves take 45–90 minutes depending on venue size
**Frequency:** Every opening shift. Non-negotiable.

**What You'll Need:**
- This checklist (printed on a clipboard or laminated at the bar)
- Access to all equipment and storage areas
- Probe thermometer for temperature checks
- Closing handover note from previous shift (T179-02) if available

**Steps:**
1. Arrive at scheduled opening prep time (typically 06:00–06:30 for 07:00 opening)
2. Read the closing handover note from the previous shift
3. Work through the checklist station by station
4. Tick each item as completed
5. Note any issues or shortages in the comments column
6. Sign off before doors open

---

## Checklist

### Date: ________ | Opening Team: ________ | Doors Open At: ________

### 1. Arrival & Safety (first 5 minutes)

- [ ] Alarm deactivated, lights on, doors secured behind you
- [ ] Walk-through safety check: no water leaks, no unusual smells, no damage overnight
- [ ] Check closing handover note — any issues flagged from last night?
- [ ] Heating/AC set to service temperature
- [ ] Background music on, volume appropriate

### 2. Espresso Machine & Grinder (15–20 minutes)

- [ ] Espresso machine turned on (if not left on overnight) — allow 20 min warm-up
- [ ] Run water through each group head for 10 seconds (flush)
- [ ] Check water softener / filtration system — salt level, flow rate
- [ ] Load house blend beans into main grinder hopper
- [ ] Dial in espresso: pull 2–3 test shots, target 18g in / 36g out / 25–28 seconds
- [ ] Taste test: flavour profile correct? Adjust grind if needed
- [ ] Decaf grinder: load beans, dial in, pull test shot
- [ ] Record grind settings: House _____ | Decaf _____
- [ ] Steam wand: purge, check pressure, wipe
- [ ] Guest/SO grinder: load beans if applicable, dial in

### 3. Bar Station Setup (10–15 minutes)

- [ ] Milk fridge stocked: whole milk, oat milk, soy milk, almond milk (check par levels)
- [ ] Milk jugs clean and positioned at bar
- [ ] Syrup bottles at bar station: check levels, wipe nozzles
- [ ] Hot chocolate powder stocked and scoop available
- [ ] Matcha powder stocked (if applicable)
- [ ] Takeaway cups (8oz, 12oz) stocked at service point
- [ ] Lids (flat and dome) stocked
- [ ] Ceramic cups and saucers: clean, dry, stacked at machine
- [ ] Glassware for iced drinks: clean and ready
- [ ] Stirrers, straws, napkins at service counter
- [ ] POS system on, delivery platform tablet on and charged
- [ ] Till float counted and confirmed: EUR _______

### 4. Food Prep Station (15–25 minutes)

- [ ] Fridge temperature check: _____ C (must be 1–5 C; if > 5 C, do not use contents — escalate)
- [ ] Freezer temperature check: _____ C (must be -18 C or below)
- [ ] Review prep quantities needed (use T179-03 Prep Quantity Guide or closing handover)
- [ ] Bread sliced and staged for service
- [ ] Avocados checked for ripeness and prepped if needed
- [ ] Salad ingredients washed, dried, and portioned
- [ ] Sandwich fillings portioned into service containers, labelled with date
- [ ] Butter portioned into ramekins or service containers
- [ ] Eggs counted and staged for cooking
- [ ] All prep containers labelled: item name, date, use-by date
- [ ] Allergen information sheet accessible at prep station

### 5. Pastry & Display Setup (5–10 minutes)

- [ ] Display case cleaned (glass inside and out)
- [ ] Display case lighting on
- [ ] Pastries set out: croissants, pain au chocolat, muffins, cakes
- [ ] Each display item has a price label and allergen indicator
- [ ] Tongs and tissue paper available for self-service (if applicable)
- [ ] Pastry count recorded for end-of-day waste tracking: _______

### 6. Front of House (10 minutes)

- [ ] Tables wiped down and set (menus, condiments if applicable)
- [ ] Chairs positioned correctly, floor swept
- [ ] Bathroom checked: clean, stocked (toilet paper, soap, hand towels)
- [ ] Entrance area clean and welcoming (doormat, signage, A-board out)
- [ ] Outdoor seating set up (if applicable and weather permits)
- [ ] Wi-Fi working (quick check from phone)

### 7. Cleaning Station Readiness

- [ ] Sanitiser spray bottles filled and positioned at bar, kitchen, and counter
- [ ] Clean cloths available (colour-coded: blue for surfaces, red for floors)
- [ ] Dishwasher on and up to temperature
- [ ] Bin bags in all bins, not overfull from last night
- [ ] Mop and bucket accessible for spills

### 8. Final Pre-Service Check

- [ ] All checklist items above completed
- [ ] Any issues or shortages noted below and communicated to manager
- [ ] Team briefing complete (if multiple staff): today's specials, 86'd items, any events
- [ ] Doors unlocked at scheduled opening time

### Issues / Shortages / Notes

| Issue | Action Taken | Escalated To |
|-------|-------------|-------------|
| *e.g., Oat milk only 4L remaining (par is 12L)* | *Added to emergency order* | *Manager* |
| *e.g., Grinder burrs feeling dull — shots channelling* | *Adjusted grind, flagged for service* | *Owner* |
| | | |

### Sign-Off

| Role | Name | Time Completed | Signature |
|------|------|---------------|-----------|
| Head Barista / Opener | | | |

---

## Common Risks

### Risk 1: Rushing the Espresso Dial-In

**Why it happens:** Running late, first customers arriving, pressure to open on time.
**How to prevent:** Schedule prep time to allow 20 minutes for machine warm-up and dial-in. Pull test shots before doing anything else. Never serve the first customer a shot you have not tasted.
**If it's already happening:** If the machine is not ready, delay opening by 5 minutes. A bad first coffee costs more than 5 minutes of revenue.

### Risk 2: Skipping Temperature Checks

**Why it happens:** "The fridge feels cold" is treated as a check. Nobody actually reads the thermometer.
**How to prevent:** Log the actual temperature reading on this checklist every single morning. If the fridge is above 5 C, do not use the contents — escalate immediately.
**If it's already happening:** Buy a digital fridge thermometer with an external display. One glance, no excuses.

### Risk 3: Opening Without Checking Handover Notes

**Why it happens:** The closer did not leave a note, or the opener does not look for it.
**How to prevent:** Make the handover note the first thing you read when you arrive. Closers must leave a note even if it says "all fine." No note = no closure.
**If it's already happening:** Create a fixed location for the handover note (e.g., taped to the espresso machine). Make it impossible to miss.

---

## Customisation Notes

**This tool is a starting point, not a rulebook.**

**Independent / Specialty Cafe:** Add dialling-in for guest/single-origin espresso and filter brew methods. Include batch brew or cold brew setup if applicable.
**Chain / Franchise:** Replace with or supplement brand-mandated opening checklists. Use this as a gap-check for items the brand checklist misses.
**Kiosk / Mobile:** Reduce to espresso, milk, cups, and a condensed food section. Your prep time is shorter but every item is more critical.
**Bakery Cafe:** Add baking schedule: oven pre-heat, dough proofing check, first bake timing. Baking prep starts earlier than bar prep.

**Making it yours:**
- Add your specific equipment names (e.g., "La Marzocca Linea PB" instead of "espresso machine")
- Adjust timing estimates to match your venue's actual prep duration
- Add photos of correct display case setup for new team members
- Include seasonal prep additions (cold brew setup in summer, soup prep in winter)

The structure matters more than the specific fields. Keep the structure, change the content.

---

## Connected Resources

**Other tools in this block:**
- T179-02 — Closing-to-Opening Handover Note (L2, template)
- T179-03 — Prep Quantity Guide (L2, template)
- T179-04 — Prep Standards Photo Guide (L3, template)
- T179-05 — Weekly Prep Review Log (L2, tracker)

**Related blocks:**
- B092

**Parent block:** B179 — Daily Prep List Protocol
**Module:** M14: Operational Rhythm & Daily Discipline
---

*Tool Version 1.0 — OIS Cafe v2*
*Built for cafe operators who value clarity, consistency, and craft.*
